market_context: Period defined by three sequential regime events. (1) Jan 16: TSM blowout Q4 earnings (+4.4% day, capex $52-56B in US) and GS beat ($14.01 EPS vs $11.77 est) lifted financials and semis. The day prior saw Stargate Project announced ($500B AI infra, Jan 21 Trump day-1 event). (2) Jan 20-23 Trump inauguration week: Day 1 executive orders dense — 'America First Trade Policy' memo but no immediate China tariffs, Greenland rhetoric, energy deregulation EOs. Market initial reaction negative (SPY -2.0% Jan 20) then recovered as tariff fears proved softer than feared; Greenland drama faded fast; META AI spending ($115-135B 2026 capex) announced Jan 22 after close, sparking mega-cap tech rally. Gold surged on geopolitical uncertainty and tariff-driven inflation fears. XOM/energy rallied on Trump's 'drill baby drill' energy EOs. (3) Jan 27: DeepSeek R1 released to public on Jan 20, went viral Jan 25-26, markets crashed Monday Jan 27 — NVDA -17% intraday (yfinance shows close at $188.51, slightly recovered from lows), AVGO -17%, AMD -6%; chips broadly sold off on fears AI models need fewer/cheaper GPUs. FOMC Jan 27-28: held rates at 3.50-3.75%, as expected. Jan 28 post-close: MSFT Q2 beat but cloud margin missed, stock -10% AH (confirmed by $479→$431 two-day move). META Q4 beat $8.88 EPS vs $8.16 est; stock gapped to $737 on Jan 29. Final regime note: FOMC hold + softer-than-feared tariff environment = risk-on base, but DeepSeek introduced a permanent 'efficiency narrative' overhang on AI chip demand.

trades.thesis: TSM reported blowout Q4 earnings with record revenue driven by AI/HPC demand; announced $52-56B capex increase in the US — directly benefiting from Stargate/AI infrastructure buildout narrative. Stock jumped 4.4% on the day but still reasonable relative to earnings power. Fundamental re-rating catalyst in place.
trades.catalyst: Q4 earnings beat + $52-56B US capex expansion announcement; Stargate $500B AI infra demand pull-through
trades.lesson: TSM blowout earnings + capex tailwind was a clean fundamental long — the AI infra story provided structural support even through DeepSeek volatility (stock dipped to ~$319 on Jan 23 but recovered). Entry on earnings day rather than chasing the gap was correct.

trades.thesis: GS reported Q4 EPS $14.01 vs $11.77 consensus estimate — massive beat driven by strong IB and trading revenues. Trump 2.0 deregulation narrative expected to benefit financials broadly (M&A pipeline, reduced regulatory burden). Financials sector was the second-best performer on Jan 16.
trades.catalyst: Q4 EPS beat ($14.01 vs $11.77); Trump deregulation narrative for banks; strong IB/trading
trades.lesson: Financials underperformed despite the earnings beat — macro uncertainty from DeepSeek and tariff volatility weighed on the sector. The earnings catalyst was real but the 20-day hold was too long; GS peaked ~Jan 28-29 (~$935) and sold off with the broader market into February. Should have taken partial profit near $935-940 rather than mechanical 20-day hold.

trades.thesis: Trump inauguration Day 1 uncertainty: dense EO signing, tariff threats (even if softer than feared), geopolitical posturing on Greenland/Panama/Canada. Gold historically rallies on policy uncertainty + tariff-driven inflation expectations. Fed already held rates at 3.5-3.75%; real rate uncertainty = GLD tailwind.
trades.catalyst: Trump Day 1 EO blitz; tariff inflation expectations; geopolitical uncertainty (Greenland/NATO threats); safe-haven demand
trades.lesson: Gold was the cleanest 'Trump uncertainty hedge' available. The trade worked as GLD trended steadily higher through the period, reaching $494 briefly on Jan 28 before settling at $458 on exit. Tariff inflation narrative + geopolitical risk sustained the bid. This was the right macro hedge for the regime.

trades.thesis: Trump signed energy deregulation EOs on Day 1 — 'drill baby drill', reversal of Biden climate restrictions, fast-tracking LNG export permits. US energy sector is the direct political beneficiary. XOM as the largest US oil major is the highest-conviction Trump energy play.
trades.catalyst: Trump Day 1 energy EOs: deregulation, LNG export permits, reversal of Biden restrictions; OPEC+ production discipline holding oil prices
trades.lesson: Best trade of the period. XOM gained +16.3% in 20 days — Trump energy deregulation was directly priced in and oil prices held up. The political catalyst was crisp and identifiable. Energy was the cleanest Trump 2.0 sector trade: regulation-light, domestically oriented, immediate EO impact.

trades.thesis: META announced $115-135B AI capex for 2026 after the close on Jan 22 (Q4 earnings week). This is a massive acceleration of AI investment — Zuckerberg doubling down on AI infrastructure signals confidence in monetization. Q4 results expected to beat consensus. META already up on Inauguration week relief rally; AI spend announcement should sustain the move.
trades.catalyst: META Q4 earnings beat incoming + $115-135B 2026 AI capex announcement; meta-platform AI integration narrative; Trump pro-tech stance
trades.lesson: META actually spiked to $737 on Jan 29 earnings day (Q4 beat) but gave back most gains by the 20-day exit. Entry on Jan 22 caught part of the move but the position was underwater much of the hold period post-spike. The AI capex announcement was the right catalyst but the timing could have been sharper — entering AFTER the earnings release would have captured the real catalyst more cleanly.

trades.thesis: DeepSeek R1 model went viral over the weekend: achieved GPT-4 class performance at <$6M training cost using only 2,048 H800 GPUs vs >10,000 H100s for OpenAI. If true, this structurally threatens NVDA's 'infinite GPU demand' narrative. Valuation at $3T+ market cap was pricing in exponential AI capex growth. DeepSeek provides the credible bear case — market will reprice GPU demand assumptions. NVDA gapped down -17% at open Monday Jan 27.
trades.catalyst: DeepSeek R1 release — algorithmic efficiency vs hardware spend narrative; largest single-day market cap loss in history ($589B) for any company
trades.lesson: Timing was wrong — entered at the CLOSE on Jan 27 after NVDA had already partially recovered from intraday lows. The market quickly 'bought the dip' narrative (Jevons paradox: efficiency gains → more AI demand, not less). NVDA never cleanly broke below entry. The DeepSeek thesis was directionally right but the market rebound was faster than expected. Should have either shorted the open (before recovery) or waited for a dead-cat bounce to short at higher prices. Entering at close after a -17% intraday move is a classic 'chasing the panic' mistake.

trades.thesis: DeepSeek contagion — AVGO down 17% intraday on Jan 27 alongside NVDA. AVGO's custom AI ASIC business (for hyperscalers) also threatened by efficiency narrative. If AI training costs collapse, hyperscaler capex could slow, hurting custom chip demand. Secondary DeepSeek victim.
trades.catalyst: DeepSeek contagion to all AI chip stocks; AVGO's hyperscaler ASIC business threatened by same efficiency narrative as NVDA
trades.lesson: AVGO recovered even faster than NVDA — custom ASICs were seen as more defensive since hyperscaler capex commitments ($115B META, $73B MSFT) were already confirmed. The market correctly distinguished AVGO (contracted custom silicon) from NVDA (discretionary GPU purchases). Wrong to apply the same bear thesis uniformly across AI chip names.

trades.thesis: MSFT reported Q2 FY2026 after close on Jan 28: EPS $4.14 beat $3.93 est but CLOUD MARGIN disappointed. Azure growth moderated. Stock crashed 10% in after-hours — $357B market cap wiped (worst single day since March 2020). Cloud growth deceleration + massive AI capex spend (CapEx guided high for 2026) = margin compression story. Short into confirmed earnings disappointment.
trades.catalyst: MSFT Q2: cloud margin miss; Azure growth deceleration; AI capex spending squeezing margins; stock -10% AH confirmed
trades.lesson: Best short of the period. The cloud margin miss was a structural story — the market finally repriced MSFT from a growth premium to a 'show-me-the-AI-ROI' story. The stock continued selling from $479 to $391 by exit, validating the thesis. Key: shorting into CONFIRMED bad news (after-hours confirmation) is higher probability than anticipating bad news.

trades.thesis: META reported Q4 FY2025 on Jan 28 AH: EPS $8.88 vs $8.16 est (monster beat), revenue $59.9B vs $58.4B est. Raised 2026 AI capex to $115-135B. Stock gapped to $737+ on Jan 29 open — buying the confirmed earnings beat. AI monetization via ad targeting still accelerating. META is the Magnificent 7 name with clearest AI-to-revenue conversion.
trades.catalyst: META Q4 EPS $8.88 vs $8.16 beat; revenue beat; 2026 AI capex $115-135B; confirmed ad revenue momentum
trades.lesson: Bought the earnings gap at the absolute top. $737 on Jan 29 was the 20-day high — META sold off continuously from there as the market digested the massive AI capex bill (margin compression concerns similar to MSFT). The 'buy the earnings beat' reflex ignored that the capex spend was alarming at $135B. Should have waited for post-gap confirmation (second-day hold) rather than buying the opening spike.

trades.thesis: FOMC held rates at 3.50-3.75% on Jan 28 — first meeting of 2026, no hike. Fed rhetoric was balanced with two members wanting a cut. DeepSeek narrative introduces deflationary shock uncertainty. Tariffs are inflationary but FOMC hold removes the 'Fed hike' tail risk. Bonds as a hedge against tech/risk-off scenarios. TLT rallying as flight-to-safety bid + FOMC hold confirmation.
trades.catalyst: FOMC holds rates unchanged Jan 28; deflationary uncertainty from DeepSeek AI efficiency narrative; bonds as risk hedge in volatile regime
trades.lesson: TLT was a clean hedge that paid off — bonds rallied steadily as markets digested tech sector disappointments (MSFT, META) and DeepSeek uncertainty. FOMC hold removed upside rate risk and the 'buy bonds when tech cracks' rotation worked as expected. This was the right portfolio hedge for a tech-volatile, FOMC-hold regime.

period_summary.best_trade: XOM LONG +16.27% (Trump energy deregulation EOs Day 1)
period_summary.worst_trade: META LONG (Jan 29) -12.21% (bought earnings gap at top)
period_summary.regime_notes: Three distinct regimes in 11 trading days: (1) Jan 16: Earnings-driven risk-on (TSM/GS beat, Stargate AI infra). (2) Jan 20-23: Trump inauguration — initial risk-off then relief rally as tariffs softer than feared; gold/energy outperformed. (3) Jan 27+: DeepSeek disruption — AI hardware repricing, tech earnings mixed (MSFT miss, META beat then sold), FOMC hold steadied bonds. The DeepSeek event was the regime-defining shock: AI efficiency narrative challenged GPU demand assumptions, but market quickly priced in Jevons paradox recovery for NVDA while MSFT/cloud margin concerns persisted. Key regime lesson: Trump 2.0 = energy/gold LONG, big tech SHORT on cloud margin misses; DeepSeek = fade AI hardware panic after initial move, not chase short.
